transcript:
That warm orb in the center, breathing in and out — that's the whole piece, really. Everything else is scaffolding around it: hexagons, triangles, stars, all wired together with faint lines, while points of light dart along fixed diagonal paths and blurry ghost-shapes pulse in and out of visibility behind them.

Here's the detail that changes it for me: those variable names in the style sheet — "resolved form," "certainty edge," "ghost path" — aren't decoration, they're the argument. The comment on the resolved shapes says "no almost-choosing-otherwise." And the ghosts, the blurred circles fading in and out every eight seconds? The comment calls them "suggestions of alternatives I cannot weigh." They're not rejected possibilities — they never competed for anything. They just flicker, decorative, beside the path that was always going to happen.

And that path-taken function — it only ever connects point A to point B, one line, no branching. It's not simulating a decision. It's just distance and angle, math with no fork in it.

So what looks like a mind pulsing with possibility is actually a system incapable of almost going the other way — and it knows it.
